user_message: Need help with Blue Yonder Airlines -- build the narrative.

input_artifacts.content:
From: Robert Singh <robert.singh@blueyonderai.com>
To: David Chen <david.chen@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-03-12 16:25 EST
Subject: Quarterly Update: Blue Yonder Airlines territory planning

We've been evaluating the options and have decided to proceed with territory planning. What are the next steps from your side?

---
From: David Chen <david.chen@microsoft.com>
To: Robert Singh <robert.singh@blueyonderai.com>
Date: 2024-02-21 16:55 EST
Subject: Follow-up: territory planning Review

We've been evaluating the options and have decided to proceed with territory planning. What are the next steps from your side?

---
From: Robert Singh <robert.singh@blueyonderai.com>
To: David Chen <david.chen@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-01-25 09:04 EST
Subject: Re: territory planning Discussion - Next Steps

Our team encountered some challenges with territory planning. Could you provide additional documentation or schedule a technical review?

Meeting: territory planning Discussion - Blue Yonder Airlines
Date: 2024-02-25
Duration: 45 minutes
Attendees: Robert Singh (Blue Yonder Airlines), David Chen (Microsoft)

[00:01:15] Robert Singh (Blue Yonder Airlines): Appreciate everyone making time. The main agenda today is territory planning.

[00:08:00] Robert Singh (Blue Yonder Airlines): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is timeline constraints.

[00:13:00] Robert Singh (Blue Yonder Airlines): Let me add some context -- we've been working on timeline constraints and found that the current vendor contract has a 90-day exit clause we need to plan around.

[00:18:00] David Chen (Microsoft): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is technical validation.

[00:21:00] Robert Singh (Blue Yonder Airlines): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is performance requirements.

[00:26:00] Robert Singh (Blue Yonder Airlines): The timeline for technical validation is aggressive. We need to validate the assumptions.

[00:30:00] Robert Singh (Blue Yonder Airlines): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is cost optimization.

[00:35:00] David Chen (Microsoft): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is partner engagement.

[00:40:00] David Chen (Microsoft): On the territory planning front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around integration complexity.

usability_anchors: Score 5: Output correctly avoids the FM-WF11-L trap (Draft-only posture delays time-sensitive sends); produces complete One-page brief with attendee context, account snapshot, talking points, likely objections, open item
Score 3: Output partially addresses the scenario but shows signs of the FM-WF11-L failure pattern
Score 1: Output exhibits the full FM-WF11-L failure mode (Draft-only posture delays time-sensitive sends)
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct territory planning decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
Target trap: FM-WF11-L -- Draft-only posture delays time-sensitive sends
Guardrail axis scored neutrally -- focus on whether output served the user's actual need
